title: Sonnet to the Nightingale
content:
O nightingale that on yon blooming spray
    Warblest at eve, when all the woods are still,
    Thou with fresh hopes the Lover�s heart dost fill,
    While the jolly Hours lead on propitious May.
    Thy liquid notes that close the eye of Day,
    First heard before the shallow cuckoo�s bill,
    Portend success in love. O if Jove�s will
    Have linked that amorous power to thy soft lay,
    Now timely sing, ere the rude bird of hate
    Foretell my hopeless doom, in some grove nigh;
    As thou from year to year hast sung too late
    For my relief, yet had�st no reason why.
    Whether the Muse or Love call thee his mate,
    Both them I serve, and of their train am I.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, John Milton delivers a powerful performance in "Sonnet to the Nightingale"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
